extraction.one_line_summary: GREIPEL won sprint after EWAN unclipped mid-sprint; PÖSTLBERGER attacked early for KOM points while in pink to defend GC.
tactical_patterns: PÖSTLBERGER in pink jersey attacked KOM sprint himself rather than delegating, suggesting teams defend minimal GC gaps personally.
ZAKARIN required full team chase after distancing on descent, confirming poor descending forces Katusha into defensive resource drain.
Quick-Step isolated GAVIRIA by forcing him to mark JUNGELS personally, preventing clean leadout despite sprint train presence.
EWAN launching sprint too early allowed mechanical incident to cost stage; positioning discipline beats raw power in 200km+ stages.

rider_signals.PÖSTLBERGER Lukas: positive: attacked KOM sprint while wearing pink to defend 4-second GC gap, showing exceptional condition after long stage
rider_signals.ZAKARIN Ilnur: negative: required full team stop-and-wait on descent after distancing himself, confirming chronic descending weakness exploitable in mountains
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: neutral: isolated by JUNGELS presence but stayed positioned, suggesting Quick-Step tactics can neutralize superior sprint trains
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: negative: unclipped during sprint launch after 200km+ stage, indicating possible fatigue-induced technical errors on long stages
rider_signals.GREIPEL André: positive: capitalized on EWAN mechanical with 200km in legs, suggesting superior endurance-sprint combination than younger rivals
